We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, City Accountant and the City Social Welfare Development Officer send demand letters to the 854 ineligible beneficiaries for the immediate refund of the illegally received subsidy totaling ₱5,124,000.00.
We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to immediately return to the DSWD the amount of ₱858,000.00 representing refunds from unqualified beneficiaries of the SAP.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Disbursing Officer/s to explain in writing why the distribution of the subsidy was made beyond the specified time frame contrary to the guidelines set in DSWD MC No. 04 and the MOA entered into by and between the DSWD and the LGU-Carcar City.

We recommended that the CSWDO facilitate communication with the concerned beneficiaries for the return of the cash assistance received from LGU as double payment which they are not anymore qualified to.

We recommended that the CSWDO explain in writing why the required photocopy of the identification card with specimen signatures of the beneficiaries were not kept on file as required under Item IX.A.1.c of DSWD MC No. 04 and Item 9, Section 1 of the MOA entered into by and between the DSWD and the City Government of Carcar.
We also recommended for the submission of the respective IDs or Barangay Certification using the prescribed format in Annex A of the said Circular in the absence of ID of the beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management direct the persons responsible for the procurement to submit a written justification for the noted overpriced medical supplies/equipment. We also recommended that Management, henceforth, be guided by the prices of Essential Emergency Medicines and Medical Devices set by DOH during this COVID-19 Health Crisis to ensure that the government achieve the best value for money in sourcing essential medical goods.

We recommended that Management explain in writing why the procurement made through Special Repeat Order exceeded 100 per cent of the total amount of the original contract contrary to Item 8.8.1.c of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020.

We recommended that the City Accountant:
Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry by debiting the Markets account and crediting Other Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses account, and
Take up the depreciation expense intended thereof.

We recommended that Management explain in writing why the unutilized amount of P140,441.50 of the BGCM which could have been used to further respond to the COVID-19 emergency was reverted back to the National Treasury even if the State of Calamity has not yet been lifted by the President of the Philippines contrary to Item 3.9 of LBC Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.

We recommended that Management submit an explanation on the utilization of the BGCM Funds for purposes other than the implementation of COVID-19 PPAs and expenses to establish the validity of the payments.

observations.managementAction: Management agreed that the City Accountant record the loan transactions in the JEV to present these accurately in the financial statements. Submit the corresponding subsidiary ledgers pertaining to the loans to the audit team to facilitate complete verification thereof.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry by debiting Advances to Contractors account and crediting Other Infrastructure Assets to take up the payment of mobilization fee which was erroneously debited to the latter account, in order to achieve fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

Require the City of Carcar to execute a yearly contract of lease embodied in a public instrument for every public market stall rental and follow the standards/guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 88-282A to ensure that interests of both parties are protected, ambiguities and/or misunderstandings are avoided.

Require the City Accountant to reclassify the amount of P507,038.70 from Business Tax to Rent Income to properly present the income account in the financial statements. Further, we recommended that the City Treasurer record the rental payments under Rent Income and not under Municipal License.
Direct the City Treasurer to enforce collection of security deposit pursuant to the Memorandum of Agreement.

Require City Accountant to provide the auditor with the estimates to be used in the computation of accumulated depreciation of the unrecognized assets, such as the salvage value and estimate life

Likewise, the City Accountant should ensure the proper recognition in the books of acquired assets guided by the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

Direct the City Accountant make the necessary adjusting entry for the transfer of amount of Advances to Contractors in the Trust Fund book  to the General Fund book. Likewise, submit a copy of the JEV made for the adjusting entry to the auditor to ensure compliance of the audit recommendation.

The GSO should issue Inventory Custodian Slips for semi-expendable items given to officials and employees. It shall likewise maintain a file of said document for proper monitoring of those semi-expendable items and persons accountable therefor.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to strengthen controls over all government property.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Accountant to compute the retention money based on the total amount due to the contractor and refrain from releasing retention money when conditions are not fully met as provided for in the revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed that the City Accountant compute the retention money based on the total amount due to the contractor and refrain releasing of retention money when conditions were not fully met.

observations.recommendation: Management agreed that the City GSO-Designate prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for approval of proper authorities. The Committee on Appraisal of Property appraise those properties and request the disposal of the same pursuant to COA rules and regulations to prevent further deterioration and to realize income from the sale thereof.

Direct management to conduct competitive bidding, use Framework Agreement, as a contractual arrangement or any alternative mode in the procurement of meals and snacks that will be more advantageous to the City following the guidelines prescribed in GPPB Resolution No. 12-2017.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to conduct competitive bidding.

observations.recommendation:
Require Management to rescind the contracts for these consultants for the period July 1 to December 31, 2019.  Renewal thereof is at the option of the new appointing HOPE. 

Ensure that contracts entered into for the engagement of services are properly signed by witnesses and must contain an attestation clause that he is not related to the appointing authority within the fourth degree of consanguinity or by affinity.
Require the different collectors to prepare, on a regular periodic basis, the Report on Unpaid Stall Rentals and submit to the City Treasurer/Accountant for monitoring/recording purposes.

The City Mayor together with the Sanggunian Panlungsod should decide on how to disburse the Prisoners Subsistence Allowance in pursuance of COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012 in order to facilitate sound internal control in adherence to the existing rules and regulations.

Likewise, the City Accountant should refrain from making reimbursements to the Officer-in-Charge Jail Warden to prevent possible misappropriation or misuse of public funds.

Direct the management to stop the granting of honoraria to the subject official/personnel due to lack of legal basis and therefore considered as irregular expenditure as defined under Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 85-55A and as amended by COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

Require the City Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to effect the correction on the account and record future transactions of similar nature in accordance with the prescribed account code and title, used the PPSAS Chart of Accounts, to properly reflect the balances in the financial statements.

The City Mayor should consider the qualifications in creating a Design and Build Committee as provided for in Item 6 of Annex G of Republic Act No. 9184 and likewise COA Circular No. 2003-004 dated July 30, 2003.

Require the City Treasurer to enforce the collection of the unpaid business taxes including penalties and surcharges, if applicable, pursuant to the rates provided in the Carcar Revenue Code of 2007. Likewise, reconcile transactions with the Business Permits Licensing Office and impose strict monitoring in the issuance of Mayor’s Permit to all businesses operating in the City.

Require the City Accountant to:

Record the progress payments to Construction in Progress – Building and Other Structures (1-07-10-030) account while construction of the project is on-going.  Transfer to their appropriate asset account shall be done once the project is 100 percent completed and Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) must be made for reclassification; and

Maintain separate SL for each project in the Construction in Progress – Building and Other Structures account for easy monitoring of the total amount to be reclassified to their respective asset account after full completion of the projects.

Direct the City Government to prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant positions in the City Accounting Office, to ensure continuity of service by its personnel and to promote operational efficiency.

Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to copy the format of the LDRRMF Investment Plan provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 and also to include in the Plan the projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended DRRM fund of the previous years, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002, to properly account the projects and activities and their implementation.

Require the City Accountant to compute for the accumulated depreciation and provide depreciation expense annually, in order to present accurate and reliable balances of fixed assets and expense    accounts in the financial statements.
Direct Management to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

Instruct Management to consider the designation of permanent members from the labor and employment, and nutrition sectors, to ensure complete and continuous representation in securing the rights and welfare of children as envisioned by RA No. 9344.

Require Management to:

the City Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly BRS, together with the supporting documents such as the monthly Bank Statements, copies of the checks, debit/credit memos, within ten (10) days after the end of each month pursuant to Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011. In case wherein monthly bank statements are not yet available, secure snapshot of the bank transactions so that the preparation of BRS can be facilitated and submitted within the required time frame.

the City Accountant to write regularly to LBP and DBP for the early submission of bank statements and enroll in their e-banking services to retrieve the balances online.

the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to make extra efforts in reconciling unreconciled cash in bank accounts. Start with balances that have little reconciling items like Trust Fund and SEF.
Instruct the BAC Secretariat to have personnel available to do the sorting of contracts and supporting documents and that advance copies of these documents be furnished the audit team.

Direct management to follow the above cited provisions and make a request for the approval of the installment schedule to COA’s Prosecution and Litigation Office, Local Services Sector, through this office, at rates authorized per COA Resolution No. 2017-021.

Require the City Assessor  to follow up the Transfer Certificate of Title of these parcels of land in favor to the City Government of Carcar. Further, we also reiterated our recommendation that the City Accountant reconcile their records with the records of the Assessor’s office, to ensure that all properties are duly accounted for and are properly recorded in the books of accounts.

Direct Management to review the existing policies regarding the operations of Carcar City College and if warranted, apply necessary changes to improve its profitability. Consideration should be given to increasing profits by assessing the current pricing structure, especially items that decrease revenues such as discounts. Operating expenses should also be kept at a minimum. Otherwise, the Carcar City College should be discontinued as a local economic enterprise and reintegrate all its functions and services into the regular LGU operations in accordance with LBC No. 111.

Require the City Assessor to make the necessary actions in updating the Schedule of Market Values (SMVs) so that appropriate ordinance will be considered and passed by the Sanggunian. 

Also, the City Assessor should undertake the general revision of the real property assessment every three years pursuant to Section 219 of RA 7160 and DILG-DOF JMC No. 2010-01.

The City Accountant and City Treasurer should see to it that documentations are complete and substantive before payments are made in order to prevent similar audit disallowances and suspensions. Submit to this office the accreditation credentials of Dux Regulus Training and Seminar Services. Failure to do so will compel this office to disallow the entire amount of P 13,000.00.  Advise employees not to attend seminars from private institutions unless these institutions are accredited by the Civil Service Commission.

Direct the Accountant that transactions pertaining to accounts with negative balances be traced to prior years’ accounting entries, subsidiary ledgers and to source documents to identify deficiencies that resulted in the negative balances Consequently, we recommend that the Accountant effect necessary adjustments in the books to present the correct account balance in the financial statements.

Revisit the provisions of the Carcar Revenue Code with the goal of updating the structure of tax rates for each well-defined business with the express provision of the Local Government Code to generate additional income.
observations.managementAction: Management has already revisited the provisions of the Carcar Revenue Code and already conducted seminars and workshops for the updates of the tax rates.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers together with all the supporting documents, as well as the monthly and quarterly reports within the prescribed time frame.  Otherwise, implement sanctions provided for in Section 122 of P.D. 1445.

observations.recommendation:
Consider the following courses of action:
Ensure that gender development is mainstreamed in the local policy-making, planning, programming and budgeting by strictly implementing the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) mechanism and that this GFPS must be fully functional by having fully functional members that have the capacity to influence and promote gender mainstreaming.

Update its gender audit and analysis of the City’s policies, programs, services and undertake in analysis of its sex-disaggregated data to provide inputs to the formulation of a GAD Code. Prioritize the formulation of the GAD Code to provide guidance to planning, program/project identification and policy making;

Develop GAD database containing information about gender mainstreaming and;

Direct the Accounting Department to maintain a separate subsidiary ledger for GAD expenditures that will facilitate reporting of fund utilization and validation thereof.

Require the GAD focal person to prepare to prepare GADs accomplishment report in the prescribed format and to include in the plan all GAD-related activities in all responsibility centers.

GAD focal person to coordinate with the City Budget Officer so that plan could be quantified and can be included in the annual budget.
Prioritize implementation of projects funded out of the 20% development fund so that the constituents of the City could maximize the benefits that could be derived from the intended projects.
Require the City Treasurer to prepare the list of taxpayers and their tax dues to serve as the City Accountant’s basis of recording the amount of tax receivables due every year.

Require the Management to make use of the free accounting system provided by the Commission on Audit through compliance of the requirements for e-NGAS installation for an efficient and effective computerized accounting system that will generate a more reliable and accurate balances of accounts.

Direct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to reconcile their records on a monthly basis and to record any adjustment found for any erroneous recoding. Also, submit a copy of the journal entry vouchers (JEV) for any adjustment made to the Auditor for verification.
Maintain an off-site back-up for the database of the computerized systems as part of a disaster recovery plan to ensure reloading in case of catastrophe and accidental error.

Direct the Accounting and Supply Sections to maintain subsidiary records (SL) on the Agency’s inventories to establish the validity of the general ledger (GL) balance and facilitate resolution of any difference that may be noted after the physical count and between both records.

Require the GSO to consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit the same, together with the original copy of the RIS to the City Accountant who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method.  Based on the SSMI, a JEV shall be drawn to record the expenditure using the appropriate accounts.

Propose to the Sangguniang Panlungsod, amendment to the Revenue Code on the rate to be applied for newly started business on printing and publication and that the City Treasury refrain from imposing tax on new businesses of this nature pending amendment to the Ordinance as enacted by the Sangguniang Panlungsod to avoid possible issues that may arise in the future.  We further recommend that concerned officials will gather information on the new and emerging businesses for possible updating of the City Code after series of public consultations and hearings.

Instruct concerned officials to revisit the provisions of the Revenue Code to come up with possible amendments that would redound to the best interest of the City and the citizenry without going beyond the provisions and requirements of the Local Government Code and its IRR.

Require the City Accountant to maintain complete and updated PPE ledger cards to support the GL account balance and assign personnel in the City Treasurer to keep corresponding property cards.  

	Likewise, create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of PPE noting down the location, person accountable and the physical condition of the properties.  Periodic reconciliation between the accounting and treasurer’s records and physical inventory shall be made.

Require the City’s Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.

Strictly observe the rules and regulations regarding the purchase of real properties and immediately cause the transfer of land titles under the name of the City Government for those parcels of land that are still under the names of its previous owners.

	Direct the City Accountant reconcile the general ledger controlling account balance with the listing made by the Assessor’s Office in order to identify the existing discrepancies and consequently make the necessary adjustments. 

	Thereafter, instruct the accounting office to start maintaining Real Property Ledger Card for each real property acquired by the City Government to effectively control and monitor any movement in the Land account and to finally present the true financial position of the agency.

Submit the written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General and the written concurrence of the Commission on Audit.

Follow the documentary requirements set forth in COA Circular No. 98-002 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the hiring of private lawyers as consultants.

Require the City to:

appropriate and charge under the General Fund the projects not eligible for funding under the 20% Development Fund; and

Create an Internal Audit Service Unit to strengthen internal control systems in compliance with Section 1 of AO No. 70 and in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 3456 as amended by R.A. No. 4177 and allocate funds for that purpose.

	the City Accountant to write regularly to LBP and DBP for the early submission of bank statements and enroll in their e-banking services to retrieve the balances online.
	the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to make extra efforts in reconciling unreconciled cash in bank accounts. Start with balances that have little reconciling items like Trust Fund and SEF.

We also recommended that the City Accountant coordinate with the City Treasurer to make a timely request from the banks for a copy of the City’s bank statements for all accounts.  In case wherein bank statements are not yet available, secure snapshots of the bank transactions so that the preparation of BRS can be facilitated and submitted within the required time frame.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to fully disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the Loans granted by the Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP), including the terms, the various promissory notes assigned in each project and the schedule of amortizations thereof.

We recommended that management require the City Accountant:
Review the output of the accounting software instead of merely relying on     what is generated by the system
Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the abnormal balances, the history thereof, and the consequences relative to the implementation of the computerization that resulted to these abnormal balances.
Implement the e-NGAS as prescribed by COA.
Adjust immediately recent accounting errors so these abnormal balances will not accumulate. Monitor the changes in each account periodically.
Request for the employment of accounting graduates in the accounting office because majority of the staff have no accounting backgrounds to record, analyze entries and to troubleshoot errors.

We recommended that Management make use of the free accounting system provided by the Commission on Audit through compliance of the requirements for e-NGAS installation for an efficient and effective computerized accounting system that will generate a more reliable and accurate balances of accounts.

We recommended that the City Accountant reconcile the accounts Real Property Tax Receivable, Special Education Tax Receivable and its reciprocal accounts – the deferred income tax.  Furthermore, the City Budget Officer should consider real property tax as one of the major source of funds in the preparation of the budget.

We recommended that the City Accountant:
Record the progress payments to Construction in Progress – Building and Other Structures (1-07-10-030) account while construction of the project is on-going.  Transfer to their appropriate asset account shall be done once the project is 100 percent completed and Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) must be made for reclassification; and
Maintain separate SL for each project in the Construction in Progress – Building and Other Structures account for easy monitoring of the total amount to be reclassified to their respective asset account after full completion of the projects.

We recommended that the City Accountant:
a)    Make the necessary adjusting entry for the transfer of amount of Advances to Contractors in the Trust Fund book, which causes its abnormal balance, to the General Fund book. Likewise, submit a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher made for the adjusting entry to the COA office to ensure compliance of the recommendation; and
Review the amounts transferred from the General Fund books to the Trust Fund books under Trust Liability-DDRMF and to make the necessary adjusting entries on the appropriations transferred for capital outlays which were not yet implemented/completed within the year.  Appropriations for capital outlays shall be made continuing in the General Fund books until the projects funded therefore are completed pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-02.

We recommended that the City Accounting personnel:
Submit evidence of the booking of expenditures to their asset accounts and the transfer of the same from the Trust fund to General fund books; likewise, make the necessary adjusting entry to properly record the transactions in pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002, and submit a copy to the Audit Team for verification;
Provide the appropriate accumulated depreciation for the periods that lapsed and likewise submit the adjusting JEV to COA for further verification; and
Record the transactions out of the LDDRM fund in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Inspection Personnel require from the supplier to submit the delivery receipts, together with the items being delivered, in order to obtain breakdown or details of the items and basis of comparison with the actual items being delivered.
We also recommended that the Accounting Personnel to make the necessary adjusting Journal Entry to reclassify the recording of  CCTVs from Information and Communication Equipment account to its proper Military, Police and Security Equipment account.

We recommended that the City Accountant include in the Notes to Financial Statements details such as the budget and utilization of current year appropriation, continuing appropriation and also the prior years’ unexpended appropriations of the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund of the City, as presented in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to properly comply with its provisions.

We recommended that the City of Carcar strictly comply with the provisions of Section 93 of the General Appropriations Act of 2018 on the submission of AAPSI report to facilitate timely validation on the actions taken by the LGU on COA findings and recommendations.

We recommended that:
The Management create IAS unit, under the administrative supervision and control of the City Mayor to institute and conduct a program of internal audit for the agency, in compliance with Budget Circular No. 2004-4 to ensure that the government spends within means, invest in the right priorities and delivers measurable results for greater transparency and accountability;
The concerned City officials submit a proposal to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for the establishment of the Internal Audit Service/Unit in the City, including the organizational structure, functional charts, personnel compliment, operating budget and funding source; and
The Management adopt the Internal Audit Manual for Local Government Units as a guide in establishing, maintaining and operating an Internal Audit Service/Unit (IAS/U), pursuant to DBM Circular No. 110 dated June 10, 2016.

We recommended that the City Government prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant positions in the City Accounting Office, to ensure continuity of service by its personnel and to promote operational efficiency.
We recommended that the City require all consultants to register with PhilGEPS and provide a PhilGEPS registration number prior to the procurement of their services.

We recommended that the Local Development Council meticulously plan the procurement of the projects out of 20% Local Development Fund and to consider those crucial developmental projects in their budget proposal to ensure their implementation within the target date.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to copy the format of the LDRRMF Investment Plan provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 and also to include in the Plan the projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRM fund of the previous years, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002, to properly account the projects and activities and their implementation.

We recommended that the Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to:
Thoroughly review the contract prior to its approval and consider amending the same to include relevant matters for the protection of the City; and
Submit immediately, the documents supporting the contracts to facilitate auditorial and technical reviews.

We recommended that Management direct the Bids and Awards Committee to see to it that before the contract signing and awarding of the contract, the required CSHP is already approved by the DOLE.  Please submit to this office, the approved CSHP of the above projects and so with the other projects not mentioned.

We recommended that Management should see to it that the Gender and  Development (GAD) Plan of the agency should fully address and adhere to the basic purpose and objectives towards the role of women in becoming a participative partner in nation building and development, with the end view of protecting their lives and rights and to live a modest life in a society.
Likewise, we recommended that the GAD Focal Point shall be responsible for conducting gender mainstreaming or self-assessment particularly on the following areas:
That the city’s plans, programs, project and activities are aligned to the     Philippine Plan for GAD;
Monitoring the use of GAD funds for the purpose for which they have been appropriated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to submit
immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers for 2017 transactions with complete
supporting documents and to keenly observe the deadline set for the submission of
reports pursuant to Sections 100, 107 and 122 of t Presidential Decree No 1445;
COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010, and COA Circular No. 2016-0004
dated September 30, 2016.
We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to submit the
complete year-end financial reports together with the necessary schedules, in
accordance with the above cited provisions, immediately for calendar year 2017,
and within the required period for the succeeding periods, for the timely verification
of the financial transactions.
We further recommend that Management cause the suspension of payment of the
salaries of the employees responsible for the delayed submission or non-submission
of the required reports until the same shall have been complied pursuant to Section
127 of the same Code.

We recommend that the City Accountant prepare and submit the monthly Bank
Reconciliation Statements, together with supporting documents such as the monthly
Bank Statements, copies of the checks, debit/credit memos, within ten (10) days
after the end of each month pursuant to Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011, to
support the Cash account balance and promptly submit to the Auditor within the
required time frame. In case wherein bank statements are not yet available, secure
snapshot of the bank transactions so that the preparation of BRS can be facilitated
and submitted within the required time frame.

We recommend that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer reconcile their
records on a monthly basis and to record any adjustment found for any erroneous
recording. Also, submit a copy of the journal entry vouchers (JEV) for any
adjustment made to the Auditor for verification.
Likewise, submit the basis on the adjustments made in the subsidiary ledger for
credit adjustments made in the three (3) funds.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to review the
transaction and make the necessary adjusting entry to record it under the
appropriate Advances to Officers and Employees account and to submit the
liquidation report together with the supporting documents of the travel.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to take-up an
adjusting entry for the reclassification of accounts and likewise submit a copy to the
COA office as proof that such were adjusted.
We further recommend that the City Accountant record transactions in accordance
with the revised chart of accounts for LGUs as provided for in COA Circular No.
2015-009 to record the liquidation under the same account where the release of fund
was booked.

We recommend that Management must require contractors to post another
performance bond that covers the unexpired portion of the projects. Henceforth,
we recommend that the LGU be vigilant on requiring the contractors especially on
projects covering more than one (1) year to complete.

We recommend that the City Mayor require all requests for time extensions be
made prior to the expiration of the contract and within thirty (30) calendar days
after such work has been commenced or after the circumstance leading to such
claim was known in order for the LGU to investigate and examine the facts that
would fully justify an extension.
Moreover, the LGU City of Carcar shall issue a written time extension order
granting the number of days work extension based on the facts gathered on their
examination in compliance to Section 10.1 of the Annex E of the Revised IRR of
R.A. 9184.

We recommend that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Officer/responsible officials
submit the monthly report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction
Management on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of
the Auditor pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that the Management consider the following courses of action:
 establish a criteria/standard that will serve as basis on how the performance of
the contractor will be measured. In addition to the right to cancel the contract, it
should also include penalties for minor deviations, Specifically, the City may
consider, among others, the reduction of the amount payable to the contractor
equivalent to the cost of services that have not been delivered;
 impose penalties for reported violation of the terms and conditions under the
contract and set a fixed amount of penalty based on the violation and/or
omissions committed by the contractor; and
 create a monitoring team and develop strategies to facilitate assessment of
efficiency of performance of the contractors.

We recommend that Management require the LCPC to prepare the Work and
Financial Plan and Budget, as well as Accomplishment Reports and Assessment &
Rating Reports, as required under DILG MC No. 2008-126 dated August 21, 2008.
We further recommend that an Inter-Agency Monitoring Task Force (IMTF) be
created to monitor and evaluate the functionality of the LCPC.

We recommend that the City Accountant and City Treasurer exert extra effort to
effect the timely release of the thirty percent (30%) share from the basic RPT to the
15 barangays of Carcar City so that these barangays can promptly implement their
respective PPAs.

We recommend that the City officials revisit articles and provisions of the Citys
existing Revenue Code of 2007 considering present business environment towards
the best interest of the public and the government. We also reiterate the
recommendation that concerned officials gather information on the new and
emerging businesses for possible updating of the City Code after a series of public
consultation and hearings.

We recommend that Management revisit the provisions of the Carcar Revenue
Code with the goal of updating the structure of tax rates for each well-defined
business with the express provision of the Local Government Code to generate
additional income.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to explain the basis in
procuring the laptop model. We also recommend that the responsible official/s
refund the amount of the laptop if this shall not be used by the office.
We further recommend that the City officials must exercise prudence in incurring
expenditures for the LGU to prevent unnecessary and excessive expenditures.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the City direct the prospective winning bidder/contractor to receive the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed on time; and direct the bidder to post Performance Bond before perfection of the contract and after the approval of the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) in accordance with Sections 37.1.3, 37.4.1 and 39 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.
We also recommend that Notice to Proceed must be dated and issued two (2) calendar days from the issuance of the contract.  It must also state the contract effectivity date in accordance with Section 37.4.1 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to refrain from granting cash advances unless the previous cash advance is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommend that the City Accountant prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements, together with supporting documents such as the monthly bank statements, copies of the checks, debit/credit memos, within ten (10) days after the end of each month pursuant to Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.   In case wherein bank statements are not yet available, secure snapshots of the bank transactions so that the preparation of BRS can be facilitated and submitted within the required time frame.

We recommend that the Office of the City Engineer concerned direct the contractors to display suitably framed and properly installed tarpaulin signboard in accordance to the prescribed form to give the people their right to information on matters of public concern, where public funds used to cover all government projects/program/activities, and follow the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Quarterly Report on Government Projects/Programs/Activities of the City must be thoroughly filled up and contain the vital information on its projects in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004.  It should also bear the signatures of the preparer, noted by the City Engineer and approved by the City Mayor.

We recommend that Management monitor and provide technical assistance to all barangays to facilitate the establishment of Materials Recovery Facilities pursuant to Section 32 of RA 9003, for an efficient and effective collection and disposal of solid wastes, and as an additional measure to mitigate health and environmental risks.

We recommend that the City Accountant further verify Check No. 7342 if the same was deposited on February 2006 and thus require further adjustment.
Moreover, we recommend that Management direct the City Treasurer to send a written notice to the payees of the stale checks and to submit the original copies of the cancelled checks.  Likewise, direct the City Accountant to make a journal entry voucher to take up the cancellation of stale checks.
Henceforth, it is further recommended that the Management strictly observe Section 59 of NGAS Manual for LGUs on proper treatment and accounting procedures for stale and cancelled checks.

We recommend that the Management direct the City Accountant to comply the requirements of the LGU to maintain a separate subsidiary ledgers for loans payable, expenditures and interest payments for each loan/borrowing availed pursuant to Section 313 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and Chapter 6, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommend that Management explain the failure to indicate the necessary terms of reference in the MOA and the non-monitoring on the project implementation in accordance with the project proposal.  Henceforth, we recommend that Management ensure that the MOA shall contain necessary terms of reference and that complete requirements are submitted and evaluated prior to the release of funds to the Non-Governmental Organizations/People’s Organizations (NGO/POs) pursuant to COA Circular 2007-001.
It is further recommended that the granting LGU monitor strictly the NGO/POs for their compliance of the utilization of the funds and the collectability of the said funds, per their Memorandum of Agreement and Project Proposal.

We recommend that the Reports of Collections and Deposits and its supporting documents be submitted daily to the Accounting Office so that the General ledgers and subsidiary ledgers will be updated to maintain the relevance and value of the financial information.

We recommend that Management require all collecting officers to maintain and update the entries in the cashbooks where all their transactions will be recorded pursuant to pertinent provisions of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume II. Further, reconciliation of the cashbooks to be maintained by the collecting officers and the subsidiary kept by the City Accounting Office should be done as required.
Direct the City Accountant to make the necessary adjustment to reflect the correct balance of accounts affected to achieve a fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Already reflected the correct balance of accounts affected.
observations.reason: The Agency made adjustments on the accounts affected.

observations.recommendation: Require the accountable officer to immediately prepare and update her cashbooks and submit the same to the audit team for verification purposes.  We also recommend for a periodic reconciliation of reports with accounting records in order to arrive at the correct and accurate balances of cash accounts as of a given period.  In this regard, we recommend that the Acting City Treasurer observe strict compliance with the above-cited guidelines in the maintenance of cashbooks.

Moreover, funds for the payment of each claim shall come from the sale of bid documents for that particular procurement contracts and not from the upcoming bidding.

observations.recommendation:
Issue the Certificate of Acceptance after the one (1) year defects liability period and after all defects and failures, if any, shall have been repaired by the Contractor and inspected by the City Engineer which in turn shall prepare the Acceptance Report.  The certificate shall be issued after submission of the Acceptance Report and the Warranty Certificate by the Contractor, however, the contractor will still be responsible for structural defects and/or failures for the completed projects as prescribed under Section 62.2.3 of the IRR of RA 9184.

Issue the Certificate of Completion only upon request of the Contractor thru the recommendation of the City Engineer by issuing the Final Inspection Report.

Furthermore, we recommend that the Performance Security of the Contractor shall remain valid until the issuance by the City Mayor of the final Certificate of Acceptance and the Warranty Security of the contractor must cover 2 to 15 years depending on the category of the project pursuant to Sec. 62.2.3.2 of the same act.

observations.recommendation:
Submit the basis for determining the contract cost per individual consultant and provide the Audit Team and the Accounting Office with a list of deliverables/ outputs per contract.

Also, direct the Personnel Officer to renew the contracts only, in case the legal, accounting, and auditing requirements of the previous contracts have been fully satisfied.

Further, instruct the City Accountant to review claims with utmost care by considering the checklist of requirements in COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 dated February 28, 2005 and the RIRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The management attached the terms of reference, specific duties and expected deliverables/outputs by their consultants,  in their claims for monetary considerations
observations.reason: Already complied with the necessary supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Head of the City Human Resource Management Office and the Office of the Administrator to require the complete personal data from the applicants, review the same before the issuance of job orders and ensure strict adherence to the terms and conditions stipulated in the Job Order contracts specifically with regards to the age limit requirement.
observations.reason: Management justified that the overaged personnel hired were operators of the heavy equipments who have the experience and skills in their field.

observations.recommendation: Direct concerned officials to support all procurement of infrastructure projects with the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) that bears the approval of the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) in conformity with Section 7 and 17 of Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184.  We also recommend that the data in the ABC to be bid shall specify each major work item as mentioned in the Section 3.g.c of Annex A of the same law.
observations.reason: Succeeding payments of infrastructure projects were supported with ABC.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Assessor to furnish the City Treasurer’s Office with the Assessment Roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties.  Based on the assessment roll the City Treasurer’s Office should prepare complete and certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year and furnish the same to the City Accountant which will serve as basis in setting up the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable.
observations.reason: Accounts had been set-up for the RPT Receivable in GF and SET Receivable is SEF in CY 2012.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the City Accountant to give priority in locating or reconciling the difference totaling P27,762,137.50 to have a fair and reliable account balances in the financial statements.

Further, require the accountant to reconcile regularly the bank accounts maintained in the books with the actual bank accounts.  Likewise, submit proof that such PNB Account still exists and that should be part of the Cash in Bank balance, otherwise, effect the necessary adjustment to reflect the correct amount of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The Local Chief Executive directed the City Accountant to locate the difference of the account balances.  Also, the management will visit the bank for any document that will prove the existence of such account.

The City Accountant traced the prior years’ financial and accounting reports/ records but could not locate the difference since there were no bank reconciliation statements.
observations.reason:
The City Accountant and the City Treasurer were still verifying the findings since no available documents were found on file.

As of December 31, 2016, the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account (1-01-02-010) had a balance of ₱714,903,983.92.

observations.recommendation: Require the City Treasurer’s Office to strictly comply with Section 55 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual, Volume II to avoid late submission of disbursement vouchers and other reports of the City.
observations.managementAction: Paid vouchers are encoded in the RCI daily but submitted to the accounting office only on the 10th day of the following month.

Management had undergone workshops, meetings and allocated budgets and implemented programs/activities relative to GAD. However, the Accounting Office has not maintained a separate subsidiary ledger for GAD expenditures, due to some errors in the system installed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the:
Local Chief Executive (i) increase the budget and maximize the utilization thereof for programs, projects and activities intended to address the concerns of Senior Citizens and Persons with Disabilities; and (ii) implement livelihood opportunities and other undertakings that shall enhance their health, physical fitness and economic and social well-being;
Head of the Senior Citizens and Persons with Disabilities Office (i) prepare/submit plans and targets for Senior Citizens and Persons with Disabilities; and (ii) monitor the monthly accomplishments as basis for the evaluation of the annual accomplishment reports; and
Management facility complies with Section 35 of the General Appropriation Act of FY 2015 to ensure the welfare of the senior citizens and differently-abled persons.
observations.managementAction: Management has increased the budget and maximized the utilization thereof, for projects, programs and activities intended to address the concerns of the Senior Citizen and PWDs.
observations.reason: Funding for the activities were consolidated with other offices.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Accounting and Supply Sections to maintain subsidiary records (SL) on the Agency’s inventories to establish the validity of the general ledger (GL) balance and facilitate resolution of any difference that may be noted after the physical count and between both records.

Require the GSO to consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit the same, together with the original copy of the RIS to the City Accountant who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method.  Based on the SSMI, a JEV shall be drawn to record the expenditure using the appropriate accounts.
observations.managementAction: Inventory items were directly issued to requisitioning office.
observations.reason: Inasmuch as the City does not have a GSO, inventory items (such as supplies and materials) are directly issued to the requisitioning office.

observations.recommendation: Require the City Accountant to maintain complete and updated PPE ledger cards to support the GL account balance and assign a personnel in the City Treasurer to keep corresponding property cards.  Likewise, create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of PPE noting down the location, person accountable and the physical condition of the properties.  Periodic reconciliation between the accounting and treasurer’s records and physical inventory shall be made.
observations.reason:
This is recurring findings and the management has not created an inventory team.

As of December 31, 2016, the PPE had a balance of P707,734,553.29.

observations.recommendation: Formulate a Solid Waste Manual of Procedures that will serve as a guide to be followed in the execution of solid waste management programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: The Solid Waste Manual of Procedures is already included in the plan.
observations.reason: The City is still waiting for the approval from EMB.

observations.recommendation:
Propose to the Sangguniang Panlungsod, amendment to the Revenue Code on the rate to be applied for newly started business on printing and publication and that the City Treasury refrain from imposing tax on new businesses of this nature pending amendment to the Ordinance as enacted by the Sangguniang Panlungsod to avoid possible issues that may arise in the future.  We further recommend that concerned officials will gather information on the new and emerging businesses for possible updating of the City Code after series of public consultations and hearings.

Instruct concerned officials to revisit the provisions of the Revenue Code to come up with possible amendments that would redound to the best interest of the City and the citizenry without going beyond the provisions and requirements of the Local Government Code and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: Revision of the Tax Revenue Code is still on-going
HRMO formulated written policies and guidelines in the hiring of Job Order Services.
observations.reason: No copy of the written policies and guidelines were submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers together with all the supporting documents, as well as the monthly and quarterly reports within the prescribed time frame.  Otherwise, implement sanctions provided for in Section 122 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive required the City Accountant to submit the vouchers with all supporting documents and all reports to COA within the prescribed time frame.
observations.reason: Submission of trial balances and other reports are still delayed and supporting schedules were not attached.

observations.recommendation:
Strictly follow the allowable limit of confidential and intelligence fund pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 99-65.

Require the return of the excess amount of cash advance made for confidential and intelligence fund to the City’s coffers.

Furnish the Auditor with copies of the transmittal letters duly received by the COA Chairman or her authorized representative pursuant to COA Circular No. 2003-003.
observations.reason: Management has not furnished COA with copies of the transmittal letters duly received by the Office of the COA Chairman.

observations.recommendation:
Strictly observe the rules and regulations regarding the purchase of real properties and immediately cause the transfer of land titles under the name of the City Government for those parcels of land that are still under the names of its previous owners.

Direct the City Accountant reconcile the general ledger controlling account balance with the listing made by the Assessor’s Office in order to identify the existing discrepancies and consequently make the necessary adjustments. 

Thereafter, instruct the accounting office to start maintaining Real Property Ledger Card for each real property acquired by the City Government to effectively control and monitor any movement in the Land account and to finally present the true financial position of the agency.
observations.managementAction: The City Assessor’s Office exerted efforts in the titling of the parcels of the land owned by the LGU.  The City accountant has maintained a subsidiary ledger for the Land account and submitted to the Assessor’s Office the source documents pertaining to the acquisition thereof.
observations.reason: Subsidiary records maintained by the Accounting Office are not yet reconciled with the Assessor’s Office.

observations.recommendation:
Submit the written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General and the written concurrence of the Commission on Audit.

Follow the documentary requirements set forth in COA Circular No. 98-002 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the hiring of private lawyers as consultants.
observations.managementAction: Management has requested a written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General.
observations.reason: Written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General was not submitted.  There is also no written concurrence from the COA.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Officer/ responsible officials submit the monthly report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: Reports submitted are only for the months of May and June 2015.

observations.recommendation: Require the GAD focal person to prepare the GAD’s accomplishment report in the prescribed report. Direct the focal person prepare the plan to include all GAD-related activities in all responsibility centers.  We also recommend that the GAD focal person coordinate with the City Budget Officer so that the plan could be quantified and can be included in the annual budget.
observations.reason: This is reiterated in Part II of this report.  The personnel in-charge admitted that she does not understand the focal point system.

observations.recommendation:
Comply strictly with the provisions of Sections 4.2.1, 4.1.2 and 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97- 002 in the granting, settlement and liquidation of Cash Advances.
Direct the City Accountant to monitor the liquidations and send demand notices to those concerned on  the outstanding cash advances granted pursuant to existing regulations to avoid misstatements of the asset and expense accounts.
observations.managementAction: Concerned officials and employees were required to settle their cash advances.
observations.reason: The balance of unliquidated cash advances had been reduced.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Accountant and concerned personnel to exert extra effort to retrieve pertinent documents and analyze the dormant accounts, ascertain the validity of claims in cases of accounts payable, and determine the existence of inventory accounts. Require the liquidation by recipients of transferred funds and settlement of disallowances and charges.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant and other concerned personnel have already exerted extra effort in retrieving the documents but could not locate any.
observations.reason: The City Accountant exerted efforts to retrieve the required documents.

observations.recommendation: Prioritize implementation of projects funded out of the 20% development fund so that the constituents of the City could maximize the benefits that could be derived from the intended projects.
observations.managementAction: Management has prioritized the implementation of the 20% development fund.  But projects scheduled for public bidding during the 4th quarter of the year will be implemented in the succeeding year.
observations.reason: Some of the projects had already been implemented.

observations.recommendation: Exert extra effort to locate the source documents in order to identify the reconciling items that caused the negative balance of the cash in vault account.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant has already exerted efforts in locating the necessary documents.  She invited the previous Municipal Treasurer to come and submit the necessary documents/ reports

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Accountant and concerned personnel to retrieve pertinent documents and analyze the dormant accounts, ascertain the validity of claims in cases of accounts payable, determine the existence of supplies inventory, and settle reciprocal accounts. The City Mayor should require the liquidation by recipients of transferred funds and settlement of disallowances and charges.  Accounts Payable without valid claimants and remained unpaid for more than two years should be reverted.  All these activities should be completed not later than December 31, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant and staff and all personnel concerned exerted extra efforts in  locating the pertinent papers necessary in the analysis of dormant accounts.  All concerned offices were required by the City Accountant to submit their respective Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued.
observations.reason: Dormant accounts are still carried in the books.

observations.recommendation: Require the City Accountant to compute for the accumulated depreciation and provide depreciation expense annually, in order to present accurate and reliable balances of fixed assets and expense    accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant has computed depreciation for PPE except for Land Improvement, and Other Transportation Equipment.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the City Accountant  to trace/locate  source documents of the said accounts for reversions and necessary adjustments   in the books and adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 141 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System should be strictly adhered to.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant and staff exerted efforts in locating source documents of the liability accounts in the prior period.
observations.reason: These accounts remained outstanding.

observations.recommendation:
Conduct proper planning before implementing any project to avoid any losses of government funds. Determine the persons accountable for the losses incurred and require payment of the amount of loss due to their negligence. 
Monitor program implementation of the planned activities.
Verify the correctness and legality of the expenses incurred since the project required that all expenses will be shouldered by the LGU as equity of the project.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal agriculture Officer has already paid part of the loss and the remaining balance was deducted from the salary of the person assigned on the monitoring of the project.

observations.recommendation:
Require concerned officials and employees to refund the amount of P486,136.00 or the amount of overprice be.  The members of the BAC should also exert effort to make confirmation of prices in the market to avoid paying for purchases with bloated prices.

Submit the required documents as per Section 25.3 of R.A. 9184 and advise the BAC members to make a thorough evaluation of all bidders by requiring all prospective bidders to submit the mandatory supporting papers.
observations.managementAction: Bidding procedures have been adopted by the agency. BAC members now make a thorough evaluation of all prospective bidders.
observations.reason: Efforts have been exerted to prevent overpricing.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned officials to refund the amount received since this violates the provisions of Local Budget Memorandum No. 2006-51 dated December 18, 2006.
observations.managementAction: Management sees to it that they will not violate again in the succeeding year.
